title: Modern Silver Rosary Necklace — Commercial Product Package (Hero Retouch + Vector Spec + Screen-Print Tech Graphic + Data-Merge Spec Sheet)
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the source brief: a "modern design for a rosary necklace," silver as the primary material, modern aesthetic, and the client's stated need for "good communication skills for revisions and feedback" (we honor this by delivering multiple review-ready variants across the package). REASONABLY ENRICHED so it becomes a runnable Adobe-connector benchmark: the raw brief literally asks to design/3D-model a piece of jewelry from scratch, which the capability sheet grades "no" (hand jewelry/3D-CAD art from scratch is out of scope — no text-to-image, no 3D, no from-scratch art). The faithful, in-scope freelance interpretation of a "jewelry_photo" category lead is therefore the production-art deliverable a real silver-jewelry seller commissions AROUND a supplied product render/shot: studio-grade commercial retouch of the supplied silver rosary photo, a clean vectorized line-art / silhouette spec for the modern design, a screen-print/halftone tech graphic for merch and stamping mockups, and a print-ready, data-merged product spec sheet built on an authored InDesign template — all packaged for client revision rounds. The CLIENT supplies the studio shot of the rosary, an authored .indd spec-sheet template with merge fields, and the SKU/material CSV; every transform below is a real, listed Adobe connector capability (no generative fill, no object removal, no compositing, no from-scratch art). image_fill_area is used only for a solid-color backdrop sweep, image_crop_and_resize only for honest reframe-crop of the canvas.
one_line_ask: Turn the client's raw studio shot of a modern silver rosary necklace into a full commercial product package: a retouched hero image on a clean sweep, a vector line-art spec, a screen-print-style halftone tech graphic, a print-ready data-merged spec-sheet PDF and a rendered wholesale line-sheet card — all silver-true and ready for revision rounds.
full_brief:
We sell handcrafted sterling silver religious jewelry and have just finalized a new MODERN-style rosary necklace (clean geometric links, minimal crucifix, brushed-silver finish). I shot it on my bench under mixed lighting and need a freelancer to build the full commercial product package we can use across our store listing, lookbook, a stamped/printed merch concept, and our wholesale line sheet.

Deliverables:
1. A retouched HERO image of the rosary: straightened, auto-toned, exposure/highlight balanced so the brushed silver reads true (not yellow-cast), the subject isolated, dropped onto a clean neutral studio sweep, cropped and resized to the exact 4:5 e-commerce frame, and given a final commercial polish (a subtle vibrance/saturation lift so the silver pops) — the flat neutral sweep already reads as a soft, distraction-free studio background. Deliver a polished JPEG and the cut-out transparent PNG.
2. A clean VECTOR line-art / silhouette of the necklace (SVG) we can scale for stamping, embroidery, and the modern logo-lockup spec sheet.
3. A screen-print / halftone TECH GRAPHIC of the rosary (single-tone, print-screen look) for a tote/tee merch mockup and our stamping vendor.
4. A print-ready PRODUCT SPEC SHEET: take our authored InDesign spec-sheet template and our SKU CSV (material, chain length, crucifix size, finish, price tiers) and data-merge it into a per-variant PDF, plus a rendered wholesale line-sheet card from our Illustrator template.

Keep everything modern, minimal, and silver-true. I will pick the final crop. Strong communication for revisions is essential.

inputs.name: rosary_studio_shot.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photorealistic amateur jewelry bench studio photograph of a MODERN sterling silver rosary necklace lying on a slightly cluttered grey workbench under mixed warm-and-cool lighting. The rosary has clean geometric oval links, small minimal smooth beads, and a flat minimalist modern crucifix — brushed/satin silver finish, not high-polish. Shot slightly tilted/crooked, mild yellow color cast from a desk lamp, real metal micro-scratches and fingerprints, faint dust, shallow but imperfect focus, candid documentary product-shot look, NOT a clean ecommerce render, NOT CGI-glossy. 4000x3000px.
inputs.realism_notes: Deliberately imperfect: crooked horizon (needs auto_straighten), warm color cast (needs color_temperature/auto_tone), real scratches/fingerprints and a messy bench background (needs subject isolation + clean sweep). This is the raw client capture, not a finished shot.

inputs.name: spec_sheet_template.indd
inputs.gen_prompt: Author a single-page InDesign product spec-sheet template (.indd/.idml) for a jewelry line sheet with REAL InDesign data-merge fields placed as text frames: <<sku>>, <<product_name>>, <<material>>, <<chain_length>>, <<crucifix_size>>, <<finish>>, <<wholesale_price>>, <<retail_price>>, and an image placeholder frame <<hero_image>>. Modern minimal layout, charcoal/silver palette, A4 portrait, brand wordmark area top-left.
inputs.realism_notes: MUST be a genuine authored .indd carrying real <<merge fields>> (the [T] tools require this — a literal-text PDF will not bind). Represents the client's existing brand line-sheet template.

inputs.name: line_sheet_template.ai
inputs.gen_prompt: Author an Adobe Illustrator template (.ai) for a one-up wholesale line-sheet card with Illustrator VARIABLES bound to text objects (sku, product_name, material, wholesale_price) and a linked-image variable for the hero. Modern minimal grid, charcoal type on white, silver accent rule.
inputs.realism_notes: Genuine authored .ai with real Illustrator Variables so document_merge_data_vector [T] can bind the CSV — not a flattened export.

inputs.name: sku_variants.csv
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a CSV of 6 rows for one modern silver rosary necklace family with columns: sku, product_name, material, chain_length, crucifix_size, finish, wholesale_price, retail_price, hero_image. Variants differ by chain length (45cm/55cm/65cm) and finish (brushed silver / oxidized silver). Header names must exactly match the InDesign <<fields>> and the Illustrator variables.
inputs.realism_notes: Column headers must exactly match the authored template field names so the [T] merge binds; hero_image column points at the produced cut-out/hero filename.

outputs.name: rosary_hero_4x5.jpg
outputs.spec: Retouched commercial hero, 4:5 (e.g. 2400x3000px) JPEG sRGB: straightened, auto-toned, neutral white balance (silver-true), subject isolated on a clean neutral solid sweep, cropped/reframed to the exact 4:5 frame (gen-free, no invented pixels), with a final vibrance/saturation polish so the silver reads crisp. The flat neutral sweep serves as the soft, distraction-free studio background (no depth-of-field blur applied — an isolated subject on a solid sweep has no distinct background layer to blur headless).

outputs.name: rosary_cutout.png
outputs.spec: Transparent-background PNG cut-out of the rosary (background removed), full-res, for listings and the spec-sheet image placeholder.

outputs.name: rosary_lineart.svg
outputs.spec: Clean vectorized line-art / silhouette SVG of the necklace for stamping/embroidery and the logo-lockup spec.

outputs.name: rosary_halftone_tech.png
outputs.spec: Single-tone screen-print / halftone tech graphic PNG of the rosary for tote/tee merch and the stamping vendor.

outputs.name: rosary_spec_sheet_merged.pdf
outputs.spec: Multi-page print-ready PDF, one page per SKU variant, produced by data-merging the authored .indd template against sku_variants.csv (material/length/finish/price + hero image).

outputs.name: rosary_line_sheet.png
outputs.spec: Rendered wholesale line-sheet card(s) from the authored .ai after Illustrator-variable data-merge against the CSV.

connector_workflow.note: Initialize upload of the client's raw studio shot into CC storage to start the pipeline.
Finalize upload; yields the asset URN/href used as the source for all editing.
Correct the crooked/tilted bench capture so the necklace sits level — first real edit, consumes step 2 output.
Auto-tone the straightened frame to balance the muddy mixed-lighting exposure baseline.
Cool the warm yellow desk-lamp cast so brushed silver reads neutral/true — core 'silver-true' requirement.
Recover blown specular highlights on the polished link edges so metal detail survives.
Isolate the necklace off the messy bench → transparent PNG cut-out (a delivered output and a reusable input).
Crop/reframe the isolated subject toward the 4:5 e-commerce aspect, centered with breathing room.
Solid-color fill (preset neutral light-grey) of the now-transparent area → clean studio sweep behind the cut-out. SOLID fill only, no compositing.
Crop and resize the swept composite to the exact roomy 4:5 target frame (e.g. 2400x3000) — gen-free reframe/resize, no invented pixels.

Final commercial polish: a subtle vibrance/saturation lift so the brushed silver reads crisp against the neutral sweep, finishing the hero JPEG. (No lens blur: the subject is already isolated on a flat solid sweep, so there is no distinct background layer to blur headless — the clean sweep itself is the soft, distraction-free background.)
Vectorize the clean cut-out into scalable line-art/silhouette SVG for stamping/embroidery/logo-lockup (reuses step 7 output).
Screen-print/halftone single-tone tech graphic from the cut-out for merch and the stamping vendor (also reuses step 7 output).
InDesign data-merge: bind the authored .indd <<fields>> + SKU CSV (image placeholder fed the cut-out) → one PDF page per variant. [T] needs the user-authored template.
Illustrator Variables data-merge on the authored .ai against the same CSV → variant line-sheet cards. [T] needs authored variables.
Export the merged .ai line-sheet to a rendered PNG line-sheet card — final deliverable of the print branch.

chaining_note: Strict output→input chain. Steps 1→2 ingest the raw shot (rosary_studio_shot.jpg → rosary_asset_urn). The retouch spine is linear: rosary_asset_urn → auto_straighten → auto_tone → color_temperature → highlights → remove_background, producing rosary_cutout.png at step 7. The hero branch continues from the cut-out: crop_and_resize → fill_area (sweep) → crop_and_resize (gen-free reframe to the exact 4:5 target) → vibrance/saturation polish, yielding rosary_hero_4x5.jpg at step 11. Three independent branches all re-consume the step-7 cut-out: vectorize → rosary_lineart.svg (12), halftone → rosary_halftone_tech.png (13), and it feeds the image placeholder of both data-merges (14, 15). The print branch chains the authored templates + CSV: merge_data_layout → spec-sheet PDF (14); merge_data_vector → merged .ai (15) → render_vector → line_sheet.png (16), the final deliverable of the print branch. No step after the initial load uses a raw input directly except the two authored templates and the CSV, which legitimately enter at the [T] merge steps; the cut-out (step 7) is the most-reused intermediate, consumed by 5 downstream steps (hero crop, vectorize, halftone, and both [T] merges). No canvas extension or generative fill is used anywhere — the 4:5 frame is achieved purely by image_crop_and_resize cropping/reframing the existing pixels (edges are cropped to hit the ratio; nothing is grown, padded, or invented).
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon flagship: 16 connector calls, 15 distinct Adobe tools, spanning 3 connector groups (A1 raster, A2 document/layout, A4 asset upload). It exercises a realistic multi-deliverable jewelry-photo production pipeline — geometric correction, multi-stage tonal/white-balance retouch for silver-true color, subject isolation, solid-sweep fill, honest reframe-crop, final vibrance/saturation polish, vectorization, screen-print halftone, and TWO authored-template data merges (InDesign [T] + Illustrator [T]) — with a heavily reused intermediate (the cut-out feeds 5 branches). Difficulty comes from honoring the capability boundaries: the literal brief ('design a rosary from scratch / 3D') is out of scope, so the agent must reinterpret it as the in-scope production-art package, sequence the white-balance/highlight steps correctly before isolation, use image_fill_area as solid sweep (not compositing) and image_crop_and_resize only as an honest reframe-crop (accepting that edges get cropped to hit the 4:5 ratio, never growing/padding the canvas), and correctly tag the two [T] merge steps as needing user-authored templates rather than fabricating layout composition.

reverify.banned_capability_check: No banned canvas-extension or generative capability is used anywhere — neither in the executable connector_workflow nor in any narrative field. The 4:5 hero frame is achieved purely by image_crop_and_resize (steps 8 and 10), a gen-free reframe-crop that crops/resizes the existing frame to the target ratio, centered on the subject, accepting that background/edges get cropped to hit 4:5 — it never grows, pads, or invents pixels in any dimension. image_fill_area (step 9) is used only as a solid-color light-grey sweep fill of the transparent area within the existing canvas (no compositing, no canvas growth). The former whole-image blur finishing step was replaced with image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation (step 11), because an isolated subject on a flat solid sweep has no distinct background layer to blur; the clean neutral sweep itself is the soft, distraction-free studio background. The two [T] merges bind genuine authored templates (spec_sheet_template.indd with <<merge fields>>, line_sheet_template.ai with Illustrator Variables), both declared in inputs[]. No canvas expansion/padding, no upscale/super-resolution, no text-to-image, no from-scratch art/logo, no object-removal-with-reconstruction. All 15 distinct tools are real, listed Adobe-connector capabilities.
reverify.notes: Fixes applied: (1) scrubbed the last stale banned-capability reference from difficulty_rationale (which named a generative reframe/expand as in-use) so every narrative field now describes only the gen-free image_crop_and_resize reframe-crop that matches the authoritative connector_workflow; (2) replaced the impossible whole-image lens-blur finishing step (step 11) — which would have softened the isolated subject with no distinct background to blur — with a subtle image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation polish, updating full_brief deliverable 1, outputs[0].spec, chaining_note, difficulty_rationale, tools_used and the step note accordingly; (3) trimmed the review-only Firefly board deep-link output (create_firefly_board, former step 17) and the now-orphaned brand_logo.png input it alone consumed, per trim policy — the six saved deliverables (hero JPEG, cut-out PNG, line-art SVG, halftone PNG, data-merged spec-sheet PDF, rendered line-sheet PNG) all remain and the task stays a substantial multi-deliverable production job. Counts reconciled: tool_call_count=16, distinct_adobe_tools=15, tools_used lists exactly the 15 unique workflow tools. Brand 'ARGENT & FAITH' is invented/fictional; no real people depicted; Adobe/Firefly names kept.
